title: caryochloa trichotoma
intro: Caryochloa trichotoma, also known as the 'Gamboge plant', is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae. The plant is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The extracts from Caryochloa trichotoma have been found to have health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and acting as an antiseptic. The plant is commonly prepared as a powder or extract, and is used in herbal remedies, often in combination with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
